codeReview.summary: The clap v4 migration and resource-first routing are mostly in place, but the implementation does not fully satisfy the milestone because it renames the canonical organization resource from `org` to `organization`, contradicting the mission command-surface contract and leaving the advertised resource tree inconsistent with the shared-state guidance.

issues.description: The parser exposes `organization` as the top-level resource instead of the mission's canonical `org`. The shared-state command-surface contract added in the same feature explicitly lists `org` as the resource name, so `toggl --help` and parsing no longer match the required grammar.

The parser tests lock in `toggl organization ...` rather than the canonical `toggl org ...`, which means the feature's own coverage reinforces the wrong public interface and would miss regressions against the required command path.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker said it followed the CLI worker procedure, but the transcript does not show the required read of the repo `.factory/library/*.md` files before code changes. The skill may need a clearer enforcement step or checklist if this precondition is important.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows reads of mission files, AGENTS.md, `src/arguments.rs`, and `src/main.rs` before edits, while the first reads of `.factory/library/*` appear only in a later session for a different feature.

summary: Review failed: the clap migration broadly works, but it shipped the wrong canonical resource name for organizations (`organization` instead of mission-defined `org`), so the new command tree does not fully satisfy the foundation command-surface contract.
